title: STAT+: Proposed ‘preclinical obesity’ diagnosis ignites global debate among experts
summary: A new way to define obesity aims to sharpen the diagnosis. Critics warn it could delay care, exclude diabetes, and deepen inequities in accessing treatment.

title: STAT+: Beyond detection: In the age of clinical AI, what counts as an FDA ‘breakthrough’ medical device?
summary: An analysis of AI-powered devices that received FDA's "breakthrough" label shows a preference for big-picture, multi-problem solutions.

title: STAT reporter goes up against radiologists to spot deepfake X-rays
summary: A STAT reporter goes up against radiologists to spot deepfake X-rays in this week's STATus Report. Watch now.

summary: Prevention is normally relegated to the periphery of national medical conferences. Not anymore.

title: Biogen, on the heels of a $5.6B buyout, turns its sights to early-stage assets
summary: In an interview, Chief Financial Officer Robin Kramer said the combined cash flow from Biogen and Apellis will not only help debt leverage ratios but also free up capacity to do other strategic deals.

title: STAT+: Large AI scribe study finds modest time savings, inconsistent use
summary: AI scribes saved doctors, across five academic medical centers, about 16 minutes per eight hours of patient care, a new study found.

summary: The FDA has often failed to share information on how it determines whether advisory committee members have financial conflicts of interest, according to a new review.

summary: Eli Lilly’s obesity pill was approved by the FDA, setting it up for fierce competition against Novo Nordisk’s new Wegovy pill.

title: FDA Approves First New Molecular Entity Under National Priority Voucher Program
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved Foundayo (orforglipron) marking the fifth approval under the Commissioner's National Priority Voucher (CNPV) pilot program.
